marketState: neutral — extreme heat wave lifting price to the fair-value ceiling while bearish 4-week seasonality and a +151 Bcf storage surplus cap medium-term upside
summary: Aug 2026 NG=F at $3.349 sits just $0.11 below the top of the current fair-value band ($3.460) while trading $0.389 above the $2.960 model midpoint — a stretched premium sustained almost entirely by an extraordinary heat event. Both the 1-7 day and 8-16 day demand windows are above the 5-year maximum range (rangePos >1.3, +30% vs 5y normal), making weather the single dominant near-term bullish catalyst; however, the 24-hour demand trend has already rolled -9.2 Bcf/d from the recent 180.4 Bcf/d peak, suggesting the heat pulse may be cresting. Seasonality is a clear medium-term headwind: the 4-week probability of a higher price is only 19% with a typical move of -1.8%, and the daily and weekly 50<200 EMA death crosses confirm the structural trend remains down even as 5m-4h momentum is bullish. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +6 Bcf injection surprise this week reinforces the supply overhang, and the fact that managed-money net positioning sits at just the 17th 5-year percentile (-84,909 net short) is the key contrarian wildcard — specs are crowded short, creating short-squeeze risk but also signalling that informed money is not fundamentally bullish at current price levels.
